title: Quiz: Classical Machine Learning Review
description: Test your understanding of classical ML concepts essential for QML

questions.question: Which ML paradigm learns from labeled input-output pairs?
questions.explanation: Supervised learning uses labeled data where each input has a corresponding target output. The model learns to map inputs to outputs.

questions.question: What does the gradient descent update rule θ = θ - η∇L minimize?
questions.explanation: Gradient descent updates parameters θ to minimize the loss function L. The negative gradient points toward decreasing loss.

questions.question: A kernel function K(x, x') computes the inner product of data in a feature space without explicitly computing the feature vectors.
questions.explanation: True! This is the 'kernel trick': K(x, x') = ⟨φ(x), φ(x')⟩ without computing φ(x) explicitly. Quantum computers can compute kernels in exponentially large Hilbert spaces.

questions.question: Which classical operation has O(n³) complexity and is a target for quantum speedup?
questions.explanation: Matrix inversion has O(n³) classical complexity. The HHL quantum algorithm offers potential exponential speedup for specific cases.

questions.question: In neural networks, what algorithm computes gradients for all parameters efficiently?
questions.explanation: Backpropagation uses the chain rule to efficiently compute gradients of the loss with respect to all parameters in a neural network.

questions.question: Which loss function is typically used for classification tasks?
questions.explanation: Cross-entropy loss is the standard choice for classification, measuring the difference between predicted probabilities and true class labels.

questions.question: Which of the following are computational bottlenecks in classical ML that quantum computers might help with? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Matrix operations, sampling, and kernel computation are computational tasks where quantum computers might offer advantages. Storing data on disk is a classical I/O operation.
